content.text: I'll work through this audit systematically, one step at a time, using direct searches only.

todos.content: Step 1: Orient in the codebase - identify project structure and Inbox model location
Step 2: Map the Inbox contract and shared pieces
Step 3: Trace inbox teardown and cleanup flow
Step 4: Audit every dependent of the Inbox contract
Step 5: Trace inbox availability/identity guards in background work
Step 6: Assess blast radius of contract change
Step 7: Produce change + verification map

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # == Schema Information
4: #
5: # Table name: inboxes
6: #
7: #  id                            :integer          not null, primary key
8: #  allow_messages_after_resolved :boolean          default(TRUE)
9: #  auto_assignment_config        :jsonb
10: #  business_name                 :string
11: #  channel_type                  :string
12: #  csat_config                   :jsonb            not null
13: #  csat_survey_enabled           :boolean          default(FALSE)
14: #  email_address                 :string
15: #  enable_auto_assignment        :boolean          default(TRUE)
16: #  enable_email_collect          :boolean          default(TRUE)
17: #  greeting_enabled              :boolean          default(FALSE)
18: #  greeting_message              :string
19: #  lock_to_single_conversation   :boolean          default(FALSE), not null
20: #  name                          :string           not null
21: #  out_of_office_message         :string
22: #  sender_name_type              :integer          default("friendly"), not null
23: #  timezone                      :string           default("UTC")
24: #  working_hours_enabled         :boolean          default(FALSE)
25: #  created_at                    :datetime         not null
26: #  updated_at                    :datetime         not null
27: #  account_id                    :integer          not null
28: #  channel_id                    :integer          not null
29: #  portal_id                     :bigint
30: #
31: # Indexes
32: #
33: #  index_inboxes_on_account_id                   (account_id)
34: #  index_inboxes_on_channel_id_and_channel_type  (channel_id,channel_type)
35: #  index_inboxes_on_portal_id                    (portal_id)
36: #
37: # Foreign Keys
38: #
39: #  fk_rails_...  (portal_id => portals.id)
40: #
41: 
42: class Inbox < ApplicationRecord
43:   include Reportable
44:   include Avatarable
45:   include OutOfOffisable
46:   include AccountCacheRevalidator
47:   include InboxAgentAvailability
48: 
49:   # Not allowing characters:
50:   validates :name, presence: true
51:   validates :account_id, presence: true
52:   validates :timezone, inclusion: { in: TZInfo::Timezone.all_identifiers }
53:   validates :out_of_office_message, length: { maximum: Limits::OUT_OF_OFFICE_MESSAGE_MAX_LENGTH }
54:   validates :greeting_message, length: { maximum: Limits::GREETING_MESSAGE_MAX_LENGTH }
55:   validate :ensure_valid_max_assignment_limit
56: 
57:   belongs_to :account
58:   belongs_to :portal, optional: true
59: 
60:   belongs_to :channel, polymorphic: true, dependent: :destroy
61: 
62:   has_many :campaigns, dependent: :destroy_async
63:   has_many :contact_inboxes, dependent: :destroy_async
64:   has_many :contacts, through: :contact_inboxes
65: 
66:   has_many :inbox_members, dependent: :destroy_async
67:   has_many :members, through: :inbox_members, source: :user
68:   has_many :conversations, dependent: :destroy_async
69:   has_many :messages, dependent: :destroy_async
70: 
71:   has_one :inbox_assignment_policy, dependent: :destroy
72:   has_one :assignment_policy, through: :inbox_assignment_policy
73:   has_one :agent_bot_inbox, dependent: :destroy_async
74:   has_one :agent_bot, through: :agent_bot_inbox
75:   has_many :webhooks, dependent: :destroy_async
76:   has_many :hooks, dependent: :destroy_async, class_name: 'Integrations::Hook'
77: 
78:   enum sender_name_type: { friendly: 0, professional: 1 }
79: 
80:   after_destroy :delete_round_robin_agents
81: 
82:   after_create_commit :dispatch_create_event
83:   after_update_commit :dispatch_update_event
84: 
85:   scope :order_by_name, -> { order('lower(name) ASC') }
86: 
87:   # Adds multiple members to the inbox
88:   # @param user_ids [Array<Integer>] Array of user IDs to add as members
89:   # @return [void]
90:   def add_members(user_ids)
91:     inbox_members.create!(user_ids.map { |user_id| { user_id: user_id } })
92:     update_account_cache
93:   end
94: 
95:   # Removes multiple members from the inbox
96:   # @param user_ids [Array<Integer>] Array of user IDs to remove
97:   # @return [void]
98:   def remove_members(user_ids)
99:     inbox_members.where(user_id: user_ids).destroy_all
100:     update_account_cache
101:   end
102: 
103:   # Sanitizes inbox name for balanced email provider compatibility
104:   # ALLOWS: /'._- and Unicode letters/numbers/emojis
105:   # REMOVES: Forbidden chars (\<>@"()) + spam-trigger symbols (!#$%&*+=?^`{|}~)
106:   def sanitized_name
107:     return default_name_for_blank_name if name.blank?
108: 
109:     sanitized = apply_sanitization_rules(name)
110:     sanitized.blank? && email? ? display_name_from_email : sanitized
111:   end
112: 
113:   def sanitized_business_name
114:     sanitize_raw_name(business_name) || sanitized_name
115:   end
116: 
117:   def sms?
118:     channel_type == 'Channel::Sms'
119:   end
120: 
121:   def facebook?
122:     channel_type == 'Channel::FacebookPage'
123:   end
124: 
125:   def instagram?
126:     (facebook? || instagram_direct?) && channel.instagram_id.present?
127:   end
128: 
129:   def instagram_direct?
130:     channel_type == 'Channel::Instagram'
131:   end
132: 
133:   def tiktok?
134:     channel_type == 'Channel::Tiktok'
135:   end
136: 
137:   def web_widget?
138:     channel_type == 'Channel::WebWidget'
139:   end
140: 
141:   def api?
142:     channel_type == 'Channel::Api'
143:   end
144: 
145:   def email?
146:     channel_type == 'Channel::Email'
147:   end
148: 
149:   def twilio?
150:     channel_type == 'Channel::TwilioSms'
151:   end
152: 
153:   def twitter?
154:     channel_type == 'Channel::TwitterProfile'
155:   end
156: 
157:   def telegram?
158:     channel_type == 'Channel::Telegram'
159:   end
160: 
161:   def whatsapp?
162:     channel_type == 'Channel::Whatsapp'
163:   end
164: 
165:   def twilio_whatsapp?
166:     channel_type == 'Channel::TwilioSms' && channel.medium == 'whatsapp'
167:   end
168: 
169:   def assignable_agents
170:     (account.users.where(id: members.select(:user_id)) + account.administrators).uniq
171:   end
172: 
173:   def active_bot?
174:     agent_bot_inbox&.active? || hooks.where(app_id: %w[dialogflow],
175:                                             status: 'enabled').count.positive?
176:   end
177: 
178:   def inbox_type
179:     channel.name
180:   end
181: 
182:   def webhook_data
183:     {
184:       id: id,
185:       name: name
186:     }
187:   end
188: 
189:   def callback_webhook_url
190:     case channel_type
191:     when 'Channel::TwilioSms'
192:       "#{ENV.fetch('FRONTEND_URL', nil)}/twilio/callback"
193:     when 'Channel::Sms'
194:       "#{ENV.fetch('FRONTEND_URL', nil)}/webhooks/sms/#{channel.phone_number.delete_prefix('+')}"
195:     when 'Channel::Line'
196:       "#{ENV.fetch('FRONTEND_URL', nil)}/webhooks/line/#{channel.line_channel_id}"
197:     when 'Channel::Whatsapp'
198:       "#{ENV.fetch('FRONTEND_URL', nil)}/webhooks/whatsapp/#{channel.phone_number}"
199:     end
200:   end
201: 
202:   def member_ids_with_assignment_capacity
203:     members.ids
204:   end
205: 
206:   def auto_assignment_v2_enabled?
207:     account.feature_enabled?('assignment_v2')
208:   end
209: 
210:   # Callers (Reauthorizable) only invoke this on a real transition, so the previous
211:   # value is always the inverse of the new boolean value.
212:   def dispatch_reauthorization_event(reauthorization_required)
213:     return if ENV['ENABLE_INBOX_EVENTS'].blank?
214: 
215:     changed_attributes = { reauthorization_required: [!reauthorization_required, reauthorization_required] }
216:     Rails.configuration.dispatcher.dispatch(INBOX_UPDATED, Time.zone.now, inbox: self, changed_attributes: changed_attributes)
217:   end
218: 
219:   private
220: 
221:   def default_name_for_blank_name
222:     email? ? display_name_from_email : ''
223:   end
224: 
225:   def sanitize_raw_name(raw)
226:     return nil if raw.blank?
227: 
228:     result = apply_sanitization_rules(raw)
229:     result.presence
230:   end
231: 
232:   def apply_sanitization_rules(name)
233:     name.gsub(/[\\<>@"!#$%&*+=?^`{|}~:;()]/, '')        # Remove forbidden chars
234:         .gsub(/[\x00-\x1F\x7F]/, ' ')                   # Replace control chars with spaces
235:         .gsub(/\A[[:punct:]]+|[[:punct:]]+\z/, '')      # Remove leading/trailing punctuation
236:         .gsub(/\s+/, ' ')                               # Normalize spaces
237:         .strip
238:   end
239: 
240:   def display_name_from_email
241:     channel.email.split('@').first.parameterize.titleize
242:   end
243: 
244:   def dispatch_create_event
245:     return if ENV['ENABLE_INBOX_EVENTS'].blank?
246: 
247:     Rails.configuration.dispatcher.dispatch(INBOX_CREATED, Time.zone.now, inbox: self)
248:   end
249: 
250:   def dispatch_update_event
251:     return if ENV['ENABLE_INBOX_EVENTS'].blank?
252: 
253:     Rails.configuration.dispatcher.dispatch(INBOX_UPDATED, Time.zone.now, inbox: self, changed_attributes: previous_changes)
254:   end
255: 
256:   def ensure_valid_max_assignment_limit
257:     # overridden in enterprise/app/models/enterprise/inbox.rb
258:   end
259: 
260:   def delete_round_robin_agents
261:     ::AutoAssignment::InboxRoundRobinService.new(inbox: self).clear_queue
262:   end
263: 
264:   def check_channel_type?
265:     ['Channel::Email', 'Channel::Api', 'Channel::WebWidget'].include?(channel_type)
266:   end
267: end
268: 
269: Inbox.prepend_mod_with('Inbox')
270: Inbox.include_mod_with('Audit::Inbox')
271: Inbox.include_mod_with('Concerns::Inbox')

(End of file - total 271 lines)

With Captain, your team can focus on complex conversations while routine questions are resolved automatically. Read more about Captain [here](https://chwt.app/captain-docs). 42: 43: ### 💬 Omnichannel Support Desk 44: 45: Chatwoot centralizes all customer conversations into one powerful inbox, no matter where your customers reach out from. It supports live chat on your website, email, Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, WhatsApp, Telegram, Line, SMS etc. 46: 47: ### 📚 Help center portal 48: 49: Publish help articles, FAQs, and guides through the built-in Help Center Portal. Enable customers to find answers on their own, reduce repetitive queries, and keep your support team focused on more complex issues. 50: 51: ### 🗂️ Other features 52: 53: #### Collaboration & Productivity 54: 55: - Private Notes and @mentions for internal team discussions. 56: - Labels to organize and categorize conversations. 57: - Keyboard Shortcuts and a Command Bar for quick navigation. 58: - Canned Responses to reply faster to frequently asked questions. 59: - Auto-Assignment to route conversations based on agent availability. 60: - Multi-lingual Support to serve customers in multiple languages. 61: - Custom Views and Filters for better inbox organization. 62: - Business Hours and Auto-Responders to manage response expectations. 63: - Teams and Automation tools for scaling support workflows. 64: - Agent Capacity Management to balance workload across the team. 65: 66: #### Customer Data & Segmentation 67: - Contact Management with profiles and interaction history. 68: - Contact Segments and Notes for targeted communication. 69: - Campaigns to proactively engage customers. 70: - Custom Attributes for storing additional customer data. 71: - Pre-Chat Forms to collect user information before starting conversations. 72: 73: #### Integrations 74: - Slack Integration to manage conversations directly from Slack. 75: - Dialogflow Integration for chatbot automation. 76: - Dashboard Apps to embed internal tools within Chatwoot. 77: - Shopify Integration to view and manage customer orders right within Chatwoot. 78: - Use Google Translate to translate messages from your customers in realtime. 79: - Create and manage Linear tickets within Chatwoot. 80: (Showing lines 1-80 of 139. 1: module Enterprise::Inbox
2:   def member_ids_with_assignment_capacity
3:     return super unless enable_auto_assignment?
4:     return filter_by_capacity(available_agents).map(&:user_id) if auto_assignment_v2_enabled?
5: 
6:     max_assignment_limit = auto_assignment_config['max_assignment_limit']
7:     overloaded_agent_ids = max_assignment_limit.present? ? get_agent_ids_over_assignment_limit(max_assignment_limit) : []
8:     super - overloaded_agent_ids
9:   end
10: 
11:   def active_bot?
12:     super || captain_active?
13:   end
14: 
15:   def captain_active?
16:     captain_assistant.present? && more_responses?
17:   end
18: 
19:   private
20: 
21:   def more_responses?
22:     account.usage_limits[:captain][:responses][:current_available].positive?
23:   end
24: 
25:   def get_agent_ids_over_assignment_limit(limit)
26:     conversations
27:       .open
28:       .where(account_id: account_id)
29:       .select(:assignee_id)
30:       .group(:assignee_id)
31:       .having("count(*) >= #{limit.to_i}")
32:       .filter_map(&:assignee_id)
33:   end
34: 
35:   def ensure_valid_max_assignment_limit
36:     return if auto_assignment_config['max_assignment_limit'].blank?
37:     return if auto_assignment_config['max_assignment_limit'].to_i.positive?
38: 
39:     errors.add(:auto_assignment_config, 'max_assignment_limit must be greater than 0')
40:   end
41: end

4:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern

6: 
7:   ALLOWED_AVATAR_CONTENT_TYPES = %w[image/jpeg image/png image/gif image/webp].freeze
8: 
9:   included do
10:     has_one_attached :avatar
11:     validate :acceptable_avatar, if: -> { avatar.changed? }
12:     after_save :fetch_avatar_from_gravatar

16:     return url_for(avatar.representation(resize_to_fill: [250, nil])) if avatar.attached? && avatar.representable?
17: 
18:     ''
19:   end

21:   def fetch_avatar_from_gravatar
22:     return unless saved_changes.key?(:email)
23:     return if email.blank?
24: 
25:     # Incase avatar_url is supplied, we don't want to fetch avatar from gravatar
26:     # So we will wait for it to be processed
27:     Avatar::AvatarFromGravatarJob.set(wait: 30.seconds).perform_later(self, email)
28:   end

31:     return unless avatar.attached?
32: 
33:     errors.add(:avatar, 'is too big') if avatar.byte_size > 15.megabytes
34: 
35:     errors.add(:avatar, 'filetype not supported') unless ALLOWED_AVATAR_CONTENT_TYPES.include?(avatar.content_type)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module OutOfOffisable
4:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
5: 
6:   OFFISABLE_ATTRS = %w[day_of_week closed_all_day open_hour open_minutes close_hour close_minutes open_all_day].freeze
7: 
8:   included do
9:     has_many :working_hours, dependent: :destroy_async
10:     after_create :create_default_working_hours
11:   end
12: 
13:   def out_of_office?
14:     working_hours_enabled? && working_hours.today.closed_now?
15:   end
16: 
17:   def working_now?
18:     !out_of_office?
19:   end
20: 
21:   def weekly_schedule
22:     working_hours.sort_by(&:day_of_week).map do |wh|
23:       {
24:         'day_of_week' => wh.day_of_week,
25:         'closed_all_day' => wh.closed_all_day,
26:         'open_hour' => wh.open_hour,
27:         'open_minutes' => wh.open_minutes,
28:         'close_hour' => wh.close_hour,
29:         'close_minutes' => wh.close_minutes,
30:         'open_all_day' => wh.open_all_day
31:       }
32:     end
33:   end
34: 
35:   # accepts an array of hashes similiar to the format of weekly_schedule
36:   #  [
37:   #    { "day_of_week"=>1,
38:   #      "closed_all_day"=>false,
39:   #      "open_hour"=>9,
40:   #      "open_minutes"=>0,
41:   #      "close_hour"=>17,
42:   #      "close_minutes"=>0,
43:   #      "open_all_day=>false" },...]
44:   def update_working_hours(params)
45:     ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
46:       params.each do |working_hour|
47:         working_hours.find_by(day_of_week: working_hour['day_of_week']).update(working_hour.slice(*OFFISABLE_ATTRS))
48:       end
49:     end
50:   end
51: 
52:   private
53: 
54:   def create_default_working_hours
55:     working_hours.create!(day_of_week: 0, closed_all_day: true, open_all_day: false)
56:     working_hours.create!(day_of_week: 1, open_hour: 9, open_minutes: 0, close_hour: 17, close_minutes: 0, open_all_day: false)
57:     working_hours.create!(day_of_week: 2, open_hour: 9, open_minutes: 0, close_hour: 17, close_minutes: 0, open_all_day: false)
58:     working_hours.create!(day_of_week: 3, open_hour: 9, open_minutes: 0, close_hour: 17, close_minutes: 0, open_all_day: false)
59:     working_hours.create!(day_of_week: 4, open_hour: 9, open_minutes: 0, close_hour: 17, close_minutes: 0, open_all_day: false)
60:     working_hours.create!(day_of_week: 5, open_hour: 9, open_minutes: 0, close_hour: 17, close_minutes: 0, open_all_day: false)
61:     working_hours.create!(day_of_week: 6, closed_all_day: true, open_all_day: false)
62:   end
63: end

1: class ApplicationRecord < ActiveRecord::Base
2:   MAX_STRING_COLUMN_LENGTH = 255
3:   MAX_TEXT_COLUMN_LENGTH = 20_000
4: 
5:   include Events::Types
6:   self.abstract_class = true
7: 
8:   before_validation :validates_column_content_length
9: 
10:   # the models that exposed in email templates through liquid
11:   def droppables
12:     %w[Account Channel Conversation Inbox User Message]
13:   end
14: 
15:   # ModelDrop class should exist in app/drops
16:   def to_drop
17:     return unless droppables.include?(self.class.name)
18: 
19:     "#{self.class.name}Drop".constantize.new(self)
20:   end
21: 
22:   private
23: 
24:   # Generic validation for all columns of type string and text
25:   # Validates the length of the column to prevent DOS via large payloads
26:   # if a custom length validation is already present, skip the validation
27:   def validates_column_content_length
28:     self.class.columns.each do |column|
29:       check_and_validate_content_length(column) if column_of_type_string_or_text?(column)
30:     end
31:   end
32: 
33:   def column_of_type_string_or_text?(column)
34:     %i[string text].include?(column.type)
35:   end
36: 
37:   def check_and_validate_content_length(column)
38:     length_validator = self.class.validators_on(column.name).find { |v| v.kind == :length }
39:     validate_content_length(column) if length_validator.blank?
40:   end
41: 
42:   def validate_content_length(column)
43:     max_length = column.type == :text ? MAX_TEXT_COLUMN_LENGTH : MAX_STRING_COLUMN_LENGTH
44:     return if self[column.name].nil? || self[column.name].length <= max_length
45: 
46:     errors.add(column.name.to_sym, "is too long (maximum is #{max_length} characters)")
47:   end
48: 
49:   def normalize_empty_string_to_nil(attrs = [])
50:     attrs.each do |attr|
51:       self[attr] = nil if self[attr].blank?
52:     end
53:   end
54: end
55: 
56: ApplicationRecord.prepend_mod_with('ApplicationRecord')

1: class DeleteObjectJob < ApplicationJob
2:   queue_as :low
3: 
4:   BATCH_SIZE = 5_000
5: 
6:   def perform(object, user = nil, ip = nil)
7:     # Pre-purge heavy associations for large objects to avoid
8:     # timeouts & race conditions due to destroy_async fan-out.
9:     purge_heavy_associations(object)
10:     object.destroy!
11:     process_post_deletion_tasks(object, user, ip)
12:   end
13: 
14:   def process_post_deletion_tasks(object, user, ip); end
15: 
16:   private
17: 
18:   def heavy_associations
19:     {
20:       Account => %i[conversations contacts inboxes reporting_events],
21:       Inbox => %i[conversations contact_inboxes reporting_events]
22:     }.freeze
23:   end
24: 
25:   def purge_heavy_associations(object)
26:     klass = heavy_associations.keys.find { |k| object.is_a?(k) }
27:     return unless klass
28: 
29:     heavy_associations[klass].each do |assoc|
30:       next unless object.respond_to?(assoc)
31: 
32:       batch_destroy(object.public_send(assoc))
33:     end
34:   end
35: 
36:   def batch_destroy(relation)
37:     relation.find_in_batches(batch_size: BATCH_SIZE) do |batch|
38:       batch.each(&:destroy!)
39:     end
40:   end
41: end
42:

1: class AccountDeletionService
2:   SOFT_DELETE_EMAIL_DOMAIN = '@chatwoot-deleted.invalid'.freeze
3: 
4:   attr_reader :account, :soft_deleted_users
5: 
6:   def initialize(account:)
7:     @account = account
8:     @soft_deleted_users = []
9:   end
10: 
11:   def perform
12:     Rails.logger.info("Deleting account #{account.id} - #{account.name} that was marked for deletion")
13: 
14:     soft_delete_orphaned_users
15:     send_compliance_notification
16:     DeleteObjectJob.perform_later(account)
17:   end
18: 
19:   private
20: 
21:   def send_compliance_notification
22:     AdministratorNotifications::AccountComplianceMailer.with(
23:       account: account,
24:       soft_deleted_users: soft_deleted_users
25:     ).account_deleted(account).deliver_later
26:   end
27: 
28:   def soft_delete_orphaned_users
29:     account.users.each do |user|
30:       # Skip users who are still associated with another account.
31:       next if user.account_users.where.not(account_id: account.id).exists?
32: 
33:       original_email = user.email
34:       user.email = soft_deleted_email_for(user)
35:       user.skip_reconfirmation!
36:       user.save!
37: 
38:       user_info = {
39:         id: user.id.to_s,
40:         original_email: original_email
41:       }
42: 
43:       soft_deleted_users << user_info
44: 
45:       Rails.logger.info("Soft deleted user #{user.id} with email #{original_email}")
46:     end
47:   end
48: 
49:   def soft_deleted_email_for(user)
50:     "#{user.id}#{SOFT_DELETE_EMAIL_DOMAIN}"
51:   end
52: end

54: class Conversation < ApplicationRecord
55:   include Labelable
56:   include LlmFormattable
57:   include AssignmentHandler
58:   include AutoAssignmentHandler
59:   include ActivityMessageHandler
60:   include UrlHelper
61:   include SortHandler
62:   include PushDataHelper
63:   include ConversationMuteHelpers
64: 
65:   validates :account_id, presence: true
66:   validates :inbox_id, presence: true
67:   validates :contact_id, presence: true
68:   before_validation :validate_additional_attributes
69:   before_validation :reset_agent_bot_when_assignee_present
70:   validates :additional_attributes, jsonb_attributes_length: true
71:   validates :custom_attributes, jsonb_attributes_length: true
72:   validates :uuid, uniqueness: true
73:   validate :validate_referer_url
74: 
75:   enum status: { open: 0, resolved: 1, pending: 2, snoozed: 3 }
76:   enum priority: { low: 0, medium: 1, high: 2, urgent: 3 }
77: 
78:   scope :unassigned, -> { where(assignee_id: nil) }
79:   scope :assigned, -> { where.not(assignee_id: nil) }
80:   scope :assigned_to, ->(agent) { where(assignee_id: agent.id) }
81:   scope :unattended, -> { where(first_reply_created_at: nil).or(where.not(waiting_since: nil)) }
82:   scope :resolvable_not_waiting, lambda { |auto_resolve_after|
83:     return none if auto_resolve_after.to_i.zero?
84: 
85:     open.where('last_activity_at < ? AND waiting_since IS NULL', Time.now.utc - auto_resolve_after.minutes)
86:   }
87:   scope :resolvable_all, lambda { |auto_resolve_after|
88:     return none if auto_resolve_after.to_i.zero?
89: 
90:     open.where('last_activity_at < ?', Time.now.utc - auto_resolve_after.minutes)

17: class Integrations::Hook < ApplicationRecord
18:   include Reauthorizable
19: 
20:   attr_readonly :app_id, :account_id, :inbox_id, :hook_type

46:   scope :inbox_hooks, -> { where(hook_type: 'inbox') }

73:     # OpenAI integration migrated to Captain::EditorService

75:     { error: 'No processor found' }
76:   end
77:

79:     return true if app.blank?
80: 
81:     flag = app.params[:feature_flag]
82:     return true unless flag

84:     account.feature_enabled?(flag)

116:     errors.add(:base, I18n.t('errors.openai.invalid_api_key'))

122:

124:     # we need setup services to create data prerequisite to functioning of the integration
125:     # in case of Leadsquared, we need to create a custom activity type for capturing conversations and transcripts
126:     # https://apidocs.leadsquared.com/create-new-activity-type-api/
127:     return unless crm_integration?

1: # == Schema Information
2: #
3: # Table name: working_hours
4: #
5: #  id             :bigint           not null, primary key
6: #  close_hour     :integer
7: #  close_minutes  :integer
8: #  closed_all_day :boolean          default(FALSE)
9: #  day_of_week    :integer          not null
10: #  open_all_day   :boolean          default(FALSE)
11: #  open_hour      :integer
12: #  open_minutes   :integer
13: #  created_at     :datetime         not null
14: #  updated_at     :datetime         not null
15: #  account_id     :bigint
16: #  inbox_id       :bigint
17: #
18: # Indexes
19: #
20: #  index_working_hours_on_account_id  (account_id)
21: #  index_working_hours_on_inbox_id    (inbox_id)
22: #
23: class WorkingHour < ApplicationRecord
24:   belongs_to :inbox
25: 
26:   before_validation :ensure_open_all_day_hours
27:   before_save :assign_account
28: 
29:   validates :open_hour,     presence: true, unless: :closed_all_day?
30:   validates :open_minutes,  presence: true, unless: :closed_all_day?
31:   validates :close_hour,    presence: true, unless: :closed_all_day?
32:   validates :close_minutes, presence: true, unless: :closed_all_day?
33: 
34:   validates :open_hour,     inclusion: 0..23, unless: :closed_all_day?
35:   validates :close_hour,    inclusion: 0..23, unless: :closed_all_day?
36:   validates :open_minutes,  inclusion: 0..59, unless: :closed_all_day?
37:   validates :close_minutes, inclusion: 0..59, unless: :closed_all_day?
38: 
39:   validate :close_after_open, unless: :closed_all_day?
40:   validate :open_all_day_and_closed_all_day
41: 
42:   def self.today
43:     # While getting the day of the week, consider the timezone as well. `first` would
44:     # return the first working hour from the list of working hours available per week.
45:     inbox = first.inbox
46:     find_by(day_of_week: Time.zone.now.in_time_zone(inbox.timezone).to_date.wday)
47:   end
48: 
49:   def open_at?(time)
50:     return false if closed_all_day?
51: 
52:     open_time = Time.zone.now.in_time_zone(inbox.timezone).change({ hour: open_hour, min: open_minutes })
53:     close_time = Time.zone.now.in_time_zone(inbox.timezone).change({ hour: close_hour, min: close_minutes })
54: 
55:     time.between?(open_time, close_time)
56:   end
57: 
58:   def open_now?
59:     inbox_time = Time.zone.now.in_time_zone(inbox.timezone)
60:     open_at?(inbox_time)
61:   end
62: 
63:   def closed_now?
64:     !open_now?
65:   end
66: 
67:   private
68: 
69:   def assign_account
70:     self.account_id = inbox.account_id
71:   end
72: 
73:   def close_after_open
74:     return unless open_hour.hours + open_minutes.minutes >= close_hour.hours + close_minutes.minutes
75: 
76:     errors.add(:close_hour, 'Closing time cannot be before opening time')
77:   end
78: 
79:   def ensure_open_all_day_hours
80:     return unless open_all_day?
81: 
82:     self.open_hour = 0
83:     self.open_minutes = 0
84:     self.close_hour = 23
85:     self.close_minutes = 59
86:   end
87: 
88:   def open_all_day_and_closed_all_day
89:     return unless open_all_day? && closed_all_day?
90: 
91:     errors.add(:base, 'open_all_day and closed_all_day cannot be true at the same time')
92:   end
93: end

1: class AutoAssignment::InboxRoundRobinService
2:   pattr_initialize [:inbox!]
3: 
4:   # called on inbox delete
5:   def clear_queue
6:     ::Redis::Alfred.delete(round_robin_key)
7:   end
8: 
9:   # called on inbox member create
10:   def add_agent_to_queue(user_id)
11:     ::Redis::Alfred.lpush(round_robin_key, user_id)
12:   end
13: 
14:   # called on inbox member delete
15:   def remove_agent_from_queue(user_id)
16:     ::Redis::Alfred.lrem(round_robin_key, user_id)
17:   end
18: 
19:   def reset_queue
20:     clear_queue
21:     add_agent_to_queue(inbox.inbox_members.map(&:user_id))
22:   end
23: 
24:   # end of queue management functions
25: 
26:   # allowed member ids = [assignable online agents supplied by the assignment service]
27:   # the values of allowed member ids should be in string format
28:   def available_agent(allowed_agent_ids: [])
29:     reset_queue unless validate_queue?
30:     user_id = get_member_from_allowed_agent_ids(allowed_agent_ids)
31:     inbox.inbox_members.find_by(user_id: user_id)&.user if user_id.present?
32:   end
33: 
34:   private
35: 
36:   def get_member_from_allowed_agent_ids(allowed_agent_ids)
37:     return nil if allowed_agent_ids.blank?
38: 
39:     user_id = queue.intersection(allowed_agent_ids).pop
40:     pop_push_to_queue(user_id)
41:     user_id
42:   end
43: 
44:   def pop_push_to_queue(user_id)
45:     return if user_id.blank?
46: 
47:     remove_agent_from_queue(user_id)
48:     add_agent_to_queue(user_id)
49:   end
50: 
51:   def validate_queue?
52:     return true if inbox.inbox_members.map(&:user_id).sort == queue.map(&:to_i).sort
53:   end
54: 
55:   def queue
56:     ::Redis::Alfred.lrange(round_robin_key)
57:   end
58: 
59:   def round_robin_key
60:     format(::Redis::Alfred::ROUND_ROBIN_AGENTS, inbox_id: inbox.id)
61:   end
62: end

<path>/Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense-benchmark/baseline/chatwoot/app/jobs/auto_assignment/assignment_job.rb</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: class AutoAssignment::AssignmentJob < ApplicationJob
2:   queue_as :default
3: 
4:   IN_FLIGHT_TTL = 5.minutes
5: 
6:   # Coalesce per inbox: at most one AssignmentJob per inbox is in-flight
7:   # (queued or running) at any time. The marker carries a token so a job only
8:   # releases its own claim (a newer job may have taken it after a TTL lapse).
9:   def self.enqueue_for_inbox(inbox_id)
10:     key = format(::Redis::Alfred::AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_IN_FLIGHT_KEY, inbox_id: inbox_id)
11:     token = SecureRandom.uuid
12:     return false unless ::Redis::Alfred.set(key, token, nx: true, ex: IN_FLIGHT_TTL)
13: 
14:     return true if perform_later(inbox_id: inbox_id, token: token)
15: 
16:     # Enqueue was halted; release our own claim so the inbox isn't gated until the TTL.
17:     ::Redis::Alfred.delete_if_equals(key, token)
18:     false
19:   rescue StandardError
20:     # Enqueue raised after we claimed the gate; release our own claim, then re-raise.
21:     ::Redis::Alfred.delete_if_equals(key, token)
22:     raise
23:   end
24: 
25:   def perform(inbox_id:, token: nil)
26:     inbox = Inbox.find_by(id: inbox_id)
27:     return unless inbox
28: 
29:     service = AutoAssignment::AssignmentService.new(inbox: inbox)
30:     assigned_count = service.perform_bulk_assignment(limit: bulk_assignment_limit)
31:     Rails.logger.info "Assigned #{assigned_count} conversations for inbox #{inbox.id}"
32:   rescue StandardError => e
33:     Rails.logger.error "Bulk assignment failed for inbox #{inbox_id}: #{e.message}"
34:     raise e if Rails.env.test?
35:   ensure
36:     release_in_flight(inbox_id, token)
37:   end
38: 
39:   private
40: 
41:   # Release the in-flight marker only if we still own it. The atomic
42:   # compare-and-delete ensures a job whose TTL lapsed can't delete a newer
43:   # job's claim. Tokenless (pre-deploy) jobs never claimed a key, so skip.
44:   def release_in_flight(inbox_id, token)
45:     return if token.nil?
46: 
47:     key = format(::Redis::Alfred::AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_IN_FLIGHT_KEY, inbox_id: inbox_id)
48:     ::Redis::Alfred.delete_if_equals(key, token)
49:   end
50: 
51:   def bulk_assignment_limit
52:     ENV.fetch('AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_BULK_LIMIT', 100).to_i
53:   end
54: end

(End of file - total 54 lines)
</content>

<path>/Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense-benchmark/baseline/chatwoot/app/services/auto_assignment/assignment_service.rb</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: class AutoAssignment::AssignmentService
2:   pattr_initialize [:inbox!]
3: 
4:   def perform_bulk_assignment(limit: 100)
5:     return 0 unless inbox.auto_assignment_v2_enabled?
6:     return 0 unless inbox.enable_auto_assignment?
7: 
8:     conversations = unassigned_conversations(limit).to_a
9:     return 0 if conversations.empty?
10:     return 0 if inbox.available_agents.empty?
11: 
12:     assigned_count = 0
13:     conversations.each do |conversation|
14:       assigned_count += 1 if perform_for_conversation(conversation)
15:     end
16:     assigned_count
17:   end
18: 
19:   private
20: 
21:   def perform_for_conversation(conversation)
22:     return false unless assignable?(conversation)
23: 
24:     agent = find_available_agent(conversation)
25:     return false unless agent
26: 
27:     assign_conversation(conversation, agent)
28:   end
29: 
30:   def assignable?(conversation)
31:     conversation.status == 'open' &&
32:       conversation.assignee_id.nil?
33:   end
34: 
35:   def unassigned_conversations(limit)
36:     scope = inbox.conversations.unassigned.open
37: 
38:     # Apply conversation priority using assignment policy if available
39:     policy = inbox.assignment_policy
40:     scope = if policy&.longest_waiting?
41:               scope.reorder(last_activity_at: :asc, created_at: :asc)
42:             else
43:               scope.reorder(created_at: :asc)
44:             end
45: 
46:     scope.limit(limit)
47:   end
48: 
49:   def find_available_agent(conversation = nil)
50:     agents = filter_agents_by_team(inbox.available_agents, conversation)
51:     return nil if agents.nil?
52: 
53:     agents = filter_agents_by_rate_limit(agents)
54:     return nil if agents.empty?
55: 
56:     round_robin_selector.select_agent(agents)
57:   end
58: 
59:   def filter_agents_by_team(agents, conversation)
60:     return agents if conversation&.team_id.blank?
61: 
62:     team = conversation.team
63:     return nil if team.blank? || team.allow_auto_assign.blank?
64: 
65:     team_member_ids = team.members.ids
66:     agents.where(user_id: team_member_ids)
67:   end
68: 
69:   def filter_agents_by_rate_limit(agents)
70:     agents.select do |agent_member|
71:       rate_limiter = build_rate_limiter(agent_member.user)
72:       rate_limiter.within_limit?
73:     end
74:   end
75: 
76:   def assign_conversation(conversation, agent)
77:     return false unless claim_and_assign(conversation, agent)
78: 
79:     conversation.reload
80: 
81:     rate_limiter = build_rate_limiter(agent)
82:     rate_limiter.track_assignment(conversation)
83: 
84:     dispatch_assignment_event(conversation, agent)
85:     true
86:   end
87: 
88:   # Atomically claim the row so two bulk runs that overlap (the in-flight gate
89:   # is best-effort and can lapse on TTL) can't both assign the same conversation.
90:   def claim_and_assign(conversation, agent)
91:     Current.executed_by = inbox.assignment_policy || inbox
92: 
93:     Conversation.transaction do
94:       locked = inbox.conversations
95:                     .where(id: conversation.id, assignee_id: nil)
96:                     .lock('FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED')
97:                     .first
98:       next false unless locked
99: 
100:       locked.update!(assignee: agent)
101:       true
102:     end
103:   ensure
104:     Current.executed_by = nil
105:   end
106: 
107:   def dispatch_assignment_event(conversation, agent)
108:     Rails.configuration.dispatcher.dispatch(
109:       Events::Types::ASSIGNEE_CHANGED,
110:       Time.zone.now,
111:       conversation: conversation,
112:       user: agent
113:     )
114:   end
115: 
116:   def build_rate_limiter(agent)
117:     AutoAssignment::RateLimiter.new(inbox: inbox, agent: agent)
118:   end
119: 
120:   def round_robin_selector
121:     @round_robin_selector ||= AutoAssignment::RoundRobinSelector.new(inbox: inbox)
122:   end
123: end
124: 
125: AutoAssignment::AssignmentService.prepend_mod_with('AutoAssignment::AssignmentService')

5:     context 'when object is heavy (Inbox)' do

16:           .to have_enqueued_job(described_class).with(inbox).on_queue('low')
17:       end
18: 
19:       it 'pre-deletes heavy associations and then destroys the object' do
20:         conv_ids = inbox.conversations.pluck(:id)
21:         ci_ids = inbox.contact_inboxes.pluck(:id)
22:         contact_ids = inbox.contacts.pluck(:id)
23:         re_ids = inbox.reporting_events.pluck(:id)
24: 
25:         described_class.perform_now(inbox)
26: 
27:         # Reload associations to ensure database state is current
28:         expect(Conversation.where(id: conv_ids).reload).to be_empty
29:         expect(ContactInbox.where(id: ci_ids).reload).to be_empty
30:         expect(ReportingEvent.where(id: re_ids).reload).to be_empty
31:         # Contacts should not be deleted for inbox destroy
32:         expect(Contact.where(id: contact_ids).reload).not_to be_empty
33:         expect { inbox.reload }.to raise_error(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound)
34:       end
35:     end

50:         conv_ids = account.conversations.pluck(:id)

54:

56:

59:         expect(Contact.where(id: contact_ids).reload).to be_empty
60:         expect(Inbox.where(id: inbox_ids).reload).to be_empty
61:         expect(ReportingEvent.where(id: re_ids).reload).to be_empty
62:         expect { account.reload }.to raise_error(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound)
63:       end

1: # This concern is primarily targeted for business models dependent on external services
2: # The auth tokens we obtained on their behalf could expire or becomes invalid.
3: # We would be aware of it until we make the API call to the service and it throws error
4: 
5: # Example:
6: # when a user changes his/her password, the auth token they provided to chatwoot becomes invalid
7: 
8: # This module helps to capture the errors into a counter and when threshold is passed would mark
9: # the object to be reauthorized. We will also send an email to the owners alerting them of the error.
10: 
11: # In the UI, we will check for the reauthorization_required? status and prompt the reauthorization flow
12: 
13: module Reauthorizable
14:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
15: 
16:   AUTHORIZATION_ERROR_THRESHOLD = 2
17: 
18:   # model attribute
19:   def reauthorization_required?
20:     ::Redis::Alfred.get(reauthorization_required_key).present?
21:   end
22: 
23:   # model attribute
24:   def authorization_error_count
25:     ::Redis::Alfred.get(authorization_error_count_key).to_i
26:   end
27: 
28:   # action to be performed when we receive authorization errors
29:   # Implement in your exception handling logic for authorization errors
30:   def authorization_error!
31:     ::Redis::Alfred.incr(authorization_error_count_key)
32:     # we are giving precendence to the authorization error threshhold defined in the class
33:     # so that channels can override the default value
34:     prompt_reauthorization! if authorization_error_count >= self.class::AUTHORIZATION_ERROR_THRESHOLD
35:   end
36: 
37:   # Performed automatically if error threshold is breached
38:   # could used to manually prompt reauthorization if auth scope changes
39:   def prompt_reauthorization!
40:     state_changed = !reauthorization_required?
41: 
42:     ::Redis::Alfred.set(reauthorization_required_key, true)
43: 
44:     reauthorization_handlers[self.class.name]&.call(self)
45: 
46:     invalidate_inbox_cache unless instance_of?(::AutomationRule)
47:     dispatch_inbox_reauthorization_event(true) if state_changed
48:   end
49: 
50:   def process_integration_hook_reauthorization_emails
51:     if slack?
52:       AdministratorNotifications::IntegrationsNotificationMailer.with(account: account).slack_disconnect.deliver_later
53:     elsif dialogflow?
54:       AdministratorNotifications::IntegrationsNotificationMailer.with(account: account).dialogflow_disconnect.deliver_later
55:     end
56:   end
57: 
58:   def send_channel_reauthorization_email(disconnect_type)
59:     AdministratorNotifications::ChannelNotificationsMailer.with(account: account).public_send(disconnect_type, inbox).deliver_later
60:   end
61: 
62:   def handle_automation_rule_reauthorization
63:     update!(active: false)
64:     AdministratorNotifications::AccountNotificationMailer.with(account: account).automation_rule_disabled(self).deliver_later
65:   end
66: 
67:   # call this after you successfully Reauthorized the object in UI
68:   def reauthorized!
69:     state_changed = reauthorization_required?
70: 
71:     ::Redis::Alfred.delete(authorization_error_count_key)
72:     ::Redis::Alfred.delete(reauthorization_required_key)
73: 
74:     invalidate_inbox_cache unless instance_of?(::AutomationRule)
75:     dispatch_inbox_reauthorization_event(false) if state_changed
76:   end
77: 
78:   private
79: 
80:   def dispatch_inbox_reauthorization_event(reauthorization_required)
81:     return unless respond_to?(:inbox)
82:     return if inbox.blank?
83: 
84:     inbox.dispatch_reauthorization_event(reauthorization_required)
85:   end
86: 
87:   def reauthorization_handlers
88:     {
89:       'Integrations::Hook' => ->(obj) { obj.process_integration_hook_reauthorization_emails },
90:       'Channel::FacebookPage' => ->(obj) { obj.send_channel_reauthorization_email(:facebook_disconnect) },
91:       'Channel::Instagram' => ->(obj) { obj.send_channel_reauthorization_email(:instagram_disconnect) },
92:       'Channel::Tiktok' => ->(obj) { obj.send_channel_reauthorization_email(:tiktok_disconnect) },
93:       'Channel::Whatsapp' => ->(obj) { obj.send_channel_reauthorization_email(:whatsapp_disconnect) },
94:       'Channel::Email' => ->(obj) { obj.send_channel_reauthorization_email(:email_disconnect) },
95:       'AutomationRule' => ->(obj) { obj.handle_automation_rule_reauthorization }
96:     }
97:   end
98: 
99:   def invalidate_inbox_cache
100:     inbox.update_account_cache if inbox.present?
101:   end
102: 
103:   def authorization_error_count_key
104:     format(::Redis::Alfred::AUTHORIZATION_ERROR_COUNT, obj_type: self.class.table_name.singularize, obj_id: id)
105:   end
106: 
107:   def reauthorization_required_key
108:     format(::Redis::Alfred::REAUTHORIZATION_REQUIRED, obj_type: self.class.table_name.singularize, obj_id: id)
109:   end
110: end

1: module AutoAssignmentHandler
2:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
3:   include Events::Types
4: 
5:   included do
6:     after_save :run_auto_assignment
7:   end
8: 
9:   private
10: 
11:   def run_auto_assignment
12:     # Assignment V2: Also trigger assignment when conversation is resolved or snoozed,
13:     # bypassing the open-only condition so the AssignmentJob can redistribute capacity.
14:     return unless conversation_status_changed_to_open? || conversation_status_changed_to_resolved_or_snoozed?
15:     return unless should_run_auto_assignment?
16: 
17:     if inbox.auto_assignment_v2_enabled?
18:       # Coalesces bursts of triggers per inbox. Fine if the job runs even when the
19:       # surrounding save rolls back: it only scans the inbox's current unassigned
20:       # conversations, so running it for an uncommitted change is harmless.
21:       AutoAssignment::AssignmentJob.enqueue_for_inbox(inbox.id)
22:     else
23:       # Use legacy assignment system
24:       # If conversation has a team, only consider team members for assignment
25:       allowed_agent_ids = team_id.present? ? team_member_ids_with_capacity : inbox.member_ids_with_assignment_capacity
26:       AutoAssignment::AgentAssignmentService.new(conversation: self, allowed_agent_ids: allowed_agent_ids).perform
27:     end
28:   end
29: 
30:   def conversation_status_changed_to_resolved_or_snoozed?
31:     inbox.auto_assignment_v2_enabled? && saved_change_to_status? && (resolved? || snoozed?)
32:   end
33: 
34:   def team_member_ids_with_capacity
35:     return [] if team.blank? || team.allow_auto_assign.blank?
36: 
37:     inbox.member_ids_with_assignment_capacity & team.members.ids
38:   end
39: 
40:   def should_run_auto_assignment?
41:     return false unless inbox.enable_auto_assignment?
42:     # Assignment V2: Resolved/snoozed conversations still have an assignee, so bypass the
43:     # assignee-blank check below. The AssignmentJob needs to run to rebalance assignments.
44:     return true if conversation_status_changed_to_resolved_or_snoozed?
45: 
46:     # run only if assignee is blank or doesn't have access to inbox
47:     assignee.blank? || inbox.members.exclude?(assignee)
48:   end
49: end

1: class Captain::InboxPendingConversationsResolutionJob < ApplicationJob
2:   CAPTAIN_INFERENCE_RESOLVE_ACTIVITY_REASON = 'no outstanding questions'.freeze
3:   CAPTAIN_INFERENCE_HANDOFF_ACTIVITY_REASON = 'pending clarification from customer'.freeze
4: 
5:   queue_as :low
6: 
7:   def perform(inbox)
8:     return if inbox.account.captain_auto_resolve_disabled?
9: 
10:     if evaluate_conversation_completion?(inbox.account)
11:       perform_with_evaluation(inbox)
12:     else
13:       perform_time_based(inbox)
14:     end
15:   ensure
16:     Current.reset
17:   end
18: 
19:   private
20: 
21:   def evaluate_conversation_completion?(account)
22:     account.feature_enabled?('captain_tasks') && account.captain_auto_resolve_evaluated?
23:   end
24: 
25:   def perform_time_based(inbox)
26:     Current.executed_by = inbox.captain_assistant
27: 
28:     resolvable_pending_conversations(inbox).each do |conversation|
29:       create_resolution_message(conversation, inbox)
30:       conversation.resolved!
31:     end
32:   end
33: 
34:   def perform_with_evaluation(inbox)
35:     Current.executed_by = inbox.captain_assistant
36: 
37:     resolvable_pending_conversations(inbox).each do |conversation|
38:       evaluation = evaluate_conversation(conversation, inbox)
39:       next unless still_resolvable_after_evaluation?(conversation)
40: 
41:       if evaluation[:complete]
42:         resolve_conversation(conversation, inbox, evaluation[:reason])
43:       else
44:         handoff_conversation(conversation, inbox, evaluation[:reason])
45:       end
46:     end
47:   end
48: 
49:   def evaluate_conversation(conversation, inbox)
50:     Captain::ConversationCompletionService.new(
51:       account: inbox.account,
52:       conversation_display_id: conversation.display_id
53:     ).perform
54:   end
55: 
56:   def resolvable_pending_conversations(inbox)
57:     inbox.conversations.pending
58:          .where('last_activity_at < ?', auto_resolve_cutoff_time)
59:          .limit(Limits::BULK_ACTIONS_LIMIT)
60:   end
61: 
62:   def still_resolvable_after_evaluation?(conversation)
63:     conversation.reload
64:     conversation.pending? && conversation.last_activity_at < auto_resolve_cutoff_time
65:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
66:     false
67:   end
68: 
69:   def auto_resolve_cutoff_time
70:     Time.now.utc - 1.hour
71:   end
72: 
73:   def resolve_conversation(conversation, inbox, reason)
74:     create_private_note(conversation, inbox, "Auto-resolved: #{reason}")
75:     create_resolution_message(conversation, inbox)
76:     conversation.with_captain_activity_context(
77:       reason: CAPTAIN_INFERENCE_RESOLVE_ACTIVITY_REASON,
78:       reason_type: :inference
79:     ) { conversation.resolved! }
80:     conversation.dispatch_captain_inference_resolved_event
81:   end
82: 
83:   def handoff_conversation(conversation, inbox, reason)
84:     create_private_note(conversation, inbox, "Auto-handoff: #{reason}")
85:     create_handoff_message(conversation, inbox)
86:     conversation.with_captain_activity_context(
87:       reason: CAPTAIN_INFERENCE_HANDOFF_ACTIVITY_REASON,
88:       reason_type: :inference
89:     ) { conversation.bot_handoff! }
90:     conversation.dispatch_captain_inference_handoff_event
91:     send_out_of_office_message_if_applicable(conversation.reload)
92:   end
93: 
94:   def send_out_of_office_message_if_applicable(conversation)
95:     # Campaign conversations should never receive OOO templates — the campaign itself
96:     # serves as the initial outreach, and OOO would be confusing in that context.
97:     return if conversation.campaign.present?
98: 
99:     ::MessageTemplates::Template::OutOfOffice.perform_if_applicable(conversation)
100:   end
101: 
102:   def create_private_note(conversation, inbox, content)
103:     conversation.messages.create!(
104:       message_type: :outgoing,
105:       private: true,
106:       sender: inbox.captain_assistant,
107:       account_id: conversation.account_id,
108:       inbox_id: conversation.inbox_id,
109:       content: content
110:     )
111:   end
112: 
113:   def create_resolution_message(conversation, inbox)
114:     I18n.with_locale(inbox.account.locale) do
115:       resolution_message = inbox.captain_assistant.config['resolution_message']
116:       conversation.messages.create!(
117:         message_type: :outgoing,
118:         account_id: conversation.account_id,
119:         inbox_id: conversation.inbox_id,
120:         content: resolution_message.presence || I18n.t('conversations.activity.auto_resolution_message'),
121:         sender: inbox.captain_assistant
122:       )
123:     end
124:   end
125: 
126:   def create_handoff_message(conversation, inbox)
127:     handoff_message = inbox.captain_assistant.config['handoff_message']
128:     return if handoff_message.blank?
129: 
130:     conversation.messages.create!(
131:       message_type: :outgoing,
132:       sender: inbox.captain_assistant,
133:       account_id: conversation.account_id,
134:       inbox_id: conversation.inbox_id,
135:       content: handoff_message,
136:       preserve_waiting_since: true
137:     )
138:   end
139: end

content.text:
## Step 1 — Orient in the codebase

Chatwoot is a Rails + Vue omnichannel support desk. Backend lives under `app/`, with an Enterprise overlay under `enterprise/app/`. The model you are reworking is `Inbox` (`app/models/inbox.rb:42`), the join point between an `Account`, a polymorphic `Channel`, `Conversation`, `ContactInbox`, `Message`, and several config/permission records. Shared pieces it builds on are in `app/models/concerns/` and the channel family is in `app/models/channel/`.

Key map for targeted lookups:
- Inbox contract: `app/models/inbox.rb`
- Included concerns: `app/models/concerns/reportable.rb`, `avatarable.rb`, `out_of_offisable.rb`, `account_cache_revalidator.rb`, `inbox_agent_availability.rb`

## Step 2 — Map the Inbox contract and shared pieces

`app/models/inbox.rb:42-271`

**Core contract**
- `belongs_to :account`, `belongs_to :portal` (optional), `belongs_to :channel, polymorphic: true, dependent: :destroy`
- `has_many :campaigns`, `:contact_inboxes`, `:inbox_members`, `:conversations`, `:messages` — all `dependent: :destroy_async`
- `has_one :inbox_assignment_policy, dependent: :destroy`
- `has_one :agent_bot_inbox, dependent: :destroy_async`
- `has_many :webhooks`, `has_many :hooks` (Integrations::Hook) — `destroy_async`
- Channel-type predicates: `sms?`, `facebook?`, `instagram?`, `api?`, `email?`, `whatsapp?`, etc. (`:117-167`)
- Identity/behaviour methods: `assignable_agents`, `active_bot?`, `inbox_type`, `webhook_data`, `callback_webhook_url`, `member_ids_with_assignment_capacity`, `auto_assignment_v2_enabled?` (`:169-208`)
- `after_destroy :delete_round_robin_agents` (`:80`, `:260-262`)
- `after_create_commit :dispatch_create_event`, `after_update_commit :dispatch_update_event` (`:82-83`)
- Mod extensions at bottom: `Inbox.prepend_mod_with('Inbox')`, `include_mod_with('Audit::Inbox')`, `include_mod_with('Concerns::Inbox')` (`:269-271`)

**Shared concerns included**
- `Reportable` (`app/models/concerns/reportable.rb:6-8`) — adds `has_many :reporting_events, dependent: :destroy`
- `Avatarable` (`app/models/concerns/avatarable.rb:9-13`) — adds `has_one_attached :avatar`, avatar validation, gravatar job
- `OutOfOffisable` (`app/models/concerns/out_of_offisable.rb:8-11`) — adds `has_many :working_hours, dependent: :destroy_async`, default working hours creation
- `AccountCacheRevalidator` (`app/models/concerns/account_cache_revalidator.rb:5-10`) — `after_commit :update_account_cache`
- `InboxAgentAvailability` (`app/models/concerns/inbox_agent_availability.rb:4-16`) — `available_agents`, `member_ids_with_assignment_capacity`

**Enterprise additions**
- `Enterprise::Concerns::Inbox` (`enterprise/app/models/enterprise/concerns/inbox.rb:4-11`) — adds `captain_inbox`, `captain_assistant`, `inbox_capacity_limits` (`dependent: :destroy`), `calls` (`dependent: :destroy_async`)
- `Enterprise::Inbox` (`enterprise/app/models/enterprise/inbox.rb:1-41`) — overrides `member_ids_with_assignment_capacity`, `active_bot?`, `ensure_valid_max_assignment_limit`
- `Enterprise::Audit::Inbox` (`enterprise/app/models/enterprise/audit/inbox.rb:4-6`) — adds `audited associated_with: :account`

**What a teammate must know before changing the contract**
- `Inbox` is the public interface for channel identity (`channel_type`, `inbox_type`, `email?`, etc.); ~40+ services branch on these methods.
- It owns both synchronous and asynchronous destroy dependencies; changing `dependent:` strategy or adding `before_destroy` will reorder cleanup with `DeleteObjectJob`.
- `avatar`, `working_hours`, `reporting_events`, and (EE) `captain_inbox`/`calls` are added via concerns, so the full association list is not visible in one file.
- It emits `INBOX_CREATED`/`INBOX_UPDATED` dispatcher events and invalidates account cache keys on every commit.

## Step 3 — Trace inbox teardown and cleanup flow

**Entry points**
1. User-driven destroy: `app/controllers/api/v1/accounts/inboxes_controller.rb:77-80` calls `DeleteObjectJob.perform_later(@inbox, Current.user, request.ip)`.
2. Account destroy cascade: `app/services/account_deletion_service.rb:16` calls `DeleteObjectJob.perform_later(account)`; `app/models/account.rb:83` has `has_many :inboxes, dependent: :destroy_async`.

**DeleteObjectJob (`app/jobs/delete_object_job.rb:1-43`)**
- For `Inbox`, it first **inline** purges heavy associations: `conversations`, `contact_inboxes`, `reporting_events` (`:18-23`, `:36-40`).
- Then calls `object.destroy!` (`:10`), which triggers model-level dependencies and callbacks.

**Model-level cleanup during/after `destroy!`**
- `belongs_to :channel, polymorphic: true, dependent: :destroy` — channel deleted synchronously (`app/models/inbox.rb:60`).
- Remaining `has_many`/`has_one` with `dependent: :destroy_async`: `campaigns`, `inbox_members`, `messages`, `agent_bot_inbox`, `webhooks`, `hooks`, `working_hours` (via OutOfOffisable), and EE `calls` (`app/models/inbox.rb:62-76`, `enterprise/app/models/enterprise/concerns/inbox.rb:4-11`).
- `has_one :inbox_assignment_policy, dependent: :destroy` — synchronous (`app/models/inbox.rb:71`).
- `Reportable` concern `has_many :reporting_events, dependent: :destroy` — synchronous, but already pre-purged by `DeleteObjectJob`.
- `Avatarable` active-storage attachment cleaned up by Active Storage.
- `after_destroy :delete_round_robin_agents` — clears Redis round-robin queue (`app/models/inbox.rb:80`, `:260-262`; key from `app/services/auto_assignment/inbox_round_robin_service.rb:59-60`).
- `AccountCacheRevalidator` `after_commit :update_account_cache` on `:destroy`.

**Enterprise post-deletion**
- `Enterprise::DeleteObjectJob.process_post_deletion_tasks` writes an `Enterprise::AuditLog` destroy entry for `Inbox` (`enterprise/app/jobs/enterprise/delete_object_job.rb:10-25`).

**Order summary**
1. `DeleteObjectJob` inline batch-deletes `conversations`, `contact_inboxes`, `reporting_events`.
2. `inbox.destroy!` deletes the channel synchronously and enqueues async destroys for the rest.
3. `after_destroy` clears the round-robin Redis queue.
4. (EE) audit log entry created.

## Step 5 — Trace how inbox availability and identity are guarded in background work

| File:line | Guard | What it checks |
|---|---|---|
| `app/jobs/auto_assignment/assignment_job.rb:26-27` | `inbox = Inbox.find_by(id: inbox_id); return unless inbox` | re-fetches by id and no-ops if inbox was deleted |
| `app/services/auto_assignment/inbox_round_robin_service.rb:51-53` | `validate_queue?` | compares Redis queue to current `inbox.inbox_members`; self-heals if membership changed |
| `app/jobs/inboxes/fetch_imap_email_inboxes_job.rb:14-22` | `should_fetch_emails?` | `inbox.account.suspended?`, `inbox.channel.imap_enabled`, `inbox.channel.reauthorization_required?`, plan gating |
| `app/services/conversations/unread_counts/broadcast_scope.rb:31-32` | `inbox_members_for` | `account.inboxes.find_by(id: inbox_id)` returns `User.none` if inbox missing |
| `app/jobs/migration/remove_stale_notifications_job.rb:14-16` | stale-id diff | deletes `Message` rows whose `inbox_id` no longer exists |
| `enterprise/app/jobs/enterprise/account/conversations_resolution_scheduler_job.rb:12-15` | scheduler guard | skips `captain_inbox.inbox` if `email?` or account auto-resolve disabled |
| `app/models/concerns/reauthorizable.rb:82-84,100-101` | nil guards | `return if inbox.blank?` before dispatch/cache |

**Notable gaps**
- `Captain::InboxPendingConversationsResolutionJob` receives the full `inbox` object; if the inbox is destroyed between scheduling and running, the job will fail on deserialization or stale object access.
- Webhook event jobs (`WhatsappEventsJob`, `TelegramEventsJob`, etc.) dereference `channel.inbox` without a nil check; a destroyed channel/inbox race will raise.

## Step 6 — Assess the blast radius of the contract change

You are changing teardown and a couple of associations. Risk ranking:

### Highest risk (will break or leave orphaned data if missed)

1. **`app/jobs/delete_object_job.rb:18-23` + `app/models/inbox.rb:60-76`** — the pre-purge list and model `dependent:` options must stay in sync. If you add a new association (e.g., EE `calls`) without adding it to `heavy_associations`, large inboxes will timeout.
2. **`app/services/auto_assignment/inbox_round_robin_service.rb:59-60`** — `after_destroy :delete_round_robin_agents` clears the Redis queue keyed by `inbox_id`. If teardown is split/async, this must still run exactly once after no more members exist.
3. **`app/models/inbox.rb:60` `belongs_to :channel, dependent: :destroy`** — channels hold provider credentials/webhooks. Changing this to `destroy_async` or removing it leaves orphaned channels and external callbacks.
4. **`app/models/concerns/out_of_offisable.rb:9` `has_many :working_hours, dependent: :destroy_async`** + **`Reportable` `reporting_events`**: these are concern-based, easy to miss when editing the model directly.
5. **Enterprise `captain_inbox`, `inbox_capacity_limits`, `calls`** (`enterprise/app/models/enterprise/concerns/inbox.rb:4-11`) — `calls` uses `destroy_async`; if calls reference recordings/conversations, orphaned records or foreign-key errors can occur.

### High risk (data integrity / background races)

6. **`app/models/inbox_member.rb:25-35`** — `after_destroy` removes from round-robin queue. During inbox teardown, member destroys run async; queue cleanup must be idempotent.
7. **`app/services/conversations/unread_counts/*`** — keyed by `inbox_id`. If inbox teardown does not clear these Redis sets, unread counts for deleted inboxes will persist.
8. **`app/jobs/migration/remove_stale_notifications_job.rb:14-16`** — exists because `messages` can be left with dead `inbox_id`. Any teardown change should make this job unnecessary, not more necessary.
9. **`app/listeners/webhook_listener.rb:121-125`** + **`app/listeners/agent_bot_listener.rb:74-76`** — rely on `inbox.agent_bot_inbox`, `inbox.channel.webhook_url`; if these associations are gone, webhooks/bot events will fail.
10. **`app/models/portal.rb:38` `has_many :inboxes, dependent: :nullify`** — portal does NOT cascade; deleting an inbox only nullifies `portal_id`. Safe, but if you change portal behavior, verify.

### Medium risk (feature behaviour / presentation)

11. **Provider services (WhatsApp, Twilio, etc.)** — many call `inbox.lock_to_single_conversation`, `inbox.contact_inboxes`, `inbox.channel`. Association changes affecting these methods will regress inbound/outbound message handling.
12. **Campaign services** — `campaign.inbox` and `inbox.inbox_type` drive one-off campaign execution.
13. **Search / reporting** — `inbox_id` filters and rollups will show stale data if associations are not cleaned.
14. **Frontend** — expects `inbox_id`, `channel_type`, `inbox_type` in payloads; presenter changes ripple to Vue components.

### Lower risk but must be re-verified

15. **Policies** — `InboxPolicy` relies on `user.assigned_inboxes`; ensure destroy still respects admin-only.
16. **Factories/specs** — `spec/models/inbox_spec.rb`, `delete_object_job_spec.rb`, dependent factories must reflect new association/destroy strategy.
